title: commiphora benguelensis
intro: Commiphora benguelensis is a plant species native to Angola and is also known as African myrrh, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Commiphora benguelensis include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and fighting infections, as well as providing relief from respiratory problems and digestive issues. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal remedy through the extraction of its resin, which is then used in traditional medicine to create various preparations such as tinctures, creams, and ointments.
